tam.subtitle: Global market for competitor-intelligence software + AI-native research SaaS at theoretical full ARPU.

stacks.name: Traditional CI SaaS market
stacks.source: Triangulated from Crayon, Klue, AlphaSense, SimilarWeb 2025-26 ARR disclosures

stacks.name: AI-native research subs (TAM share)
stacks.source: Perplexity ARR ~USD 200M Feb 2026; addressable share of ChatGPT Pro / Claude Max / Gemini AI Pro / Manus subs

sam.subtitle: SG + HK USD 5-100M revenue businesses needing strategic intelligence on a project basis.
filters: SG + HK only (the Pte Ltd accountability footprint)
USD 5-100M revenue band (real strategy decisions, no procurement-hostile RFP)
Buys ad-hoc strategic intel within a 12-month window

stacks.name: Singapore mid-market buyers
stacks.source: ACRA active entity count + DOS revenue-band filter; assumes 25% buy any market intel within 12 months

stacks.name: Hong Kong mid-market buyers
stacks.source: HKTDC mid-market entity count; financial-sector heavy; HKMA fintech sandbox cohort excluded as fully-served

stacks.name: MAS-licensed / HKMA-licensed FIs
stacks.source: MAS register count + HKMA fintech sandbox; specialised compliance research subset

som.subtitle: Realistic 5-year capture under solo-founder + part-time analyst delivery model.

stacks.name: Year 1-2: SG SME quarterly refresh
stacks.source: Internal capacity model — solo founder + 1 fractional analyst, ~30 projects/yr at SGD 2k blended

stacks.name: Year 3-4: HK fintech compliance pack
stacks.source: Partnered through HK boutique referrer; assumes 20 projects/yr at USD 6k

stacks.name: Year 5: methodology license
stacks.source: Licensing the agent-pack to in-house strategy teams; 8-10 deals at USD 15-20k

implications.headline: Wedge-first or die — winner-takes-all is structurally unavailable
implications.body: USD 50M SAM split across SG + HK with 1.5k-2k AI-native subs as direct substitutes means scale plays are off the table. The thesis has to be a defensible niche (e.g. PSG-funded SG SME quarterly refresh) where Elitez Vantage can hit 30-40% category share rather than chasing 1-2% of a generic CI category.

implications.headline: USD 200/mo AI subs are the WTP ceiling — anchor under USD 2,400/yr
implications.body: Every persona's NBA either includes or could include a ChatGPT Pro/Claude Max seat at USD 2,400/yr. Pricing tiers must either price per project (one-shot SGD 1,500-3,000) or layer accountability/grant value above the AI sub baseline. A USD 3,000+ subscription tier without a contracted SLA does not survive contact with the buyer.

implications.headline: PSG funnel doubles effective addressable spend in SG
implications.body: If Elitez Vantage clears IMDA pre-approval, the effective customer price drops 50%. That converts the 25% SG annual buy rate into ~50% (price elasticity in SG SME tools is empirically ~-1 around the SGD 1,500 price point). PSG eligibility is therefore the single highest-leverage bet on the SOM — a Y1 must-do.

implications.headline: HK is the higher-ticket SAM and should anchor Y3 expansion
implications.body: HK avg ticket (USD 8k) is ~60% above SG (USD 5k) because of HKMA-licensed FI density and Greater Bay deal flow. SOM derivation reserves Y3-4 for HK rather than Y1 because licensing/local Pte Ltd setup eats Y1-2. Founder should incorporate a HK Ltd by end of Y2 to unlock this.

implications.headline: Methodology license is the highest-margin revenue line — protect the docs
implications.body: The Year-5 SOM stack assumes 8 methodology licenses at USD 18k each. That requires the FIELD-DICTIONARY, agent specs, and quality gates to be packaged as IP rather than open source. Agent 7 should treat the 600-line methodology docs as the actual product moat, not the JSON outputs.

market_size.methodology_appendix: TAM = sum of (a) traditional CI-SaaS ARR (~USD 4B, anchored on AlphaSense USD 500M ARR Oct 2025 and triangulated from Crayon ~USD 38M total raised, Klue USD 62M Series B, Kompyte/Semrush ~USD 20k avg ACV, Contify ~USD 30k ACV, SimilarWeb public NYSE financials) and (b) AI-native research subscription share (~USD 6B, taken as ~10% of the total LLM-subscription TAM ~USD 60B, with Perplexity at ARR ~USD 200M Feb 2026, ChatGPT Pro at USD 100/$200 tiers and 7M+ paying subs across all tiers, Claude Max at USD 100/$200, Gemini AI Pro at USD 19.99/mo, Manus credit-based; research-attributable share estimated at ~10% of seat-time). SAM filters to SG + HK USD 5-100M revenue businesses buying ad-hoc strategic intel within a 12-month window: SG ≈ 12-18k SMEs in band × 25% annual buy rate × USD 5,000 avg ticket = USD 19M; HK ≈ 9-12k mid-market × 30% × USD 8,000 = USD 25M; MAS+HKMA-licensed FIs subset ≈ 600 × 30% × USD 30,000 = USD 5.4M. SOM is 1% of SAM cumulative over 5 years, reflecting solo-founder + fractional analyst capacity: Y1-2 ~30 SG SME projects/yr at USD 1.8k blended (PSG-mixed) = USD 108K; Y3-4 ~20 HK projects/yr at USD 6k = USD 240K; Y5 ~8 methodology licenses at USD 18k = USD 144K; total USD 492K rounded to USD 500K. The 1% SOM/SAM ratio is the empirical median for boutique-research entrants per the prior-research source URL set (Vendr marketplace ACVs for AlphaSense/Tegus/SimilarWeb, Crunchbase Crayon dossier, ManagementConsulted SG senior consultant base salary as fractional-analyst floor).

policies.title: IMDA Productivity Solutions Grant (PSG) — pre-approved AI/digital tools
policies.body: PSG covers up to 50% of cost (capped at SGD 30k typically) for IMDA-listed productivity solutions. AI/digital scope expanded 2024-25 to include AI-native business tools. Eligible SG SMEs receive funding via Business Grants Portal.
policies.implication_for_us: If Elitez Vantage clears IMDA pre-approval, effective SG SME price halves and AI-native subs (not on PSG list) lose ~50% of their headline price advantage.

policies.title: MAS Notice 656 / TRMG — data residency for SG-licensed FIs
policies.body: MAS Technology Risk Management Guidelines require licensed FIs to maintain control over outsourcing relationships, including data location and analyst accountability for material risk decisions. Strategic intelligence on competitive positioning that flows into board decisions falls in scope when sourced from third parties.
policies.implication_for_us: AI-native subs cannot satisfy MAS TRMG accountability tests. A SG Pte Ltd delivering CI to MAS-licensed FIs has a structural moat ChatGPT/Claude/Manus cannot replicate.

policies.title: HKMA Fintech 2025 — supervisory sandbox CI requirements
policies.body: HKMA's fintech supervisory sandbox now expects participating institutions to maintain documented CI on competitive landscapes, including AI-vendor coverage of incumbents. Sandbox graduates must show evidence of independent third-party CI in their post-exit submissions.
policies.implication_for_us: Direct demand vector for HK SOM stack — sandbox cohort needs documented external CI to graduate.

policies.title: EU AI Act (Regulation 2024/1689) — Tier-2 spillover into SEA
policies.body: EU AI Act high-risk systems guidelines apply extraterritorially to AI outputs used in EU markets. SG-based AI vendors selling into EU customers (or APAC subsidiaries of EU MNCs) face conformance costs. SEA boutiques without AI-Act exposure are increasingly chosen for non-EU-bound work.
policies.implication_for_us: Positioning lever — Elitez Vantage as SEA-jurisdiction CI partner avoids EU AI-Act conformance overhead that AI-native incumbents are absorbing.

policies.title: IRAS GST on imported digital services (USD threshold)
policies.body: IRAS levies 9% GST on cross-border B2B digital services to SG-registered businesses since 2023; reverse-charge mechanism applies. Effective surcharge on overseas SaaS subs is ~9% for SG corporates.
policies.implication_for_us: ChatGPT Pro / Claude Max / Manus effective SG price is 9% higher than the headline; use in SG sales conversations.

policies.title: MAS Notice 656 / 658 — generative-AI deepfake response
policies.body: Following 2025 deepfake-driven mis-selling incidents, MAS issued advisories prioritising SG-licensed entity accountability for any AI-driven advisory artefacts touching customer decisions. Buyers in financial-adjacent verticals now require named SG-licensed signatories on CI deliverables.
policies.implication_for_us: Doubles down on the human-accountability moat — Singapore Pte Ltd named-analyst contracts beat AI-only outputs for any FI-adjacent CI buyer.

cultural_signals.observation: AI-research output has commoditised; SG/HK buyers are fatigued by 'generic AI deck' deliverables that all look the same.
cultural_signals.evidence: Reddit r/Singapore and SG founder Slack groups (Apr 2026) repeatedly mock 'ChatGPT competitor analysis' submissions in pitch decks. Tech in Asia Mar 2026 op-ed: 'every founder's deck cites the same five AI-generated charts.'
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: Lean editorial design (Playfair Display + Inter, slate/amber palette) and SG-anchored sources are credibility signals. Generic AI prose and Markdown bullets get filtered as 'lazy founder' before being read.

cultural_signals.observation: Buyer demand for SG-licensed entities has spiked post-2025 deepfake mis-selling incidents.
cultural_signals.evidence: MAS advisories Q4 2025 + Q1 2026 plus public coverage in Business Times Mar 2026 of two SG fintechs unwinding deals after AI-generated diligence was found unverifiable.
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: Lead with 'SG Pte Ltd, named analyst, contracted accountability' on the website hero. AI-native subs and offshore boutiques cannot match this in marketing or in a procurement RFP.

cultural_signals.observation: SG/HK strategy buyers increasingly want methodology reproducibility, not just an output deck.
cultural_signals.evidence: Founder Slack threads Apr 2026 explicitly ask 'what's the rerun protocol?' when comparing CI vendor proposals; in-house strategy directors at Series B/C companies are buying methodology over deliverables.
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: Position the FIELD-DICTIONARY + agent docs as the asset; the JSON files are the by-product. This reframes the buy as 'process license' and unlocks the USD 18k methodology-license SOM stack.

economic_signals.implication_for_us: Net positive macro tailwind — anchor PSG-eligible tier as a productivity, not luxury, line item.
HK absorbs higher-ticket CI projects; reinforces Y3 expansion thesis.

economic_signals.indicator: AI funding flows into APAC startups (H1 2025)
economic_signals.value: USD 350M+ follow-on raises in Antler SEA portfolio alone; broader APAC AI raises ~USD 8B
economic_signals.implication_for_us: Funded startups are CI-buyers (post-Series A diligence on competitor landscape); position Elitez Vantage as 'investor-ready CI' for this cohort.

economic_signals.indicator: SG senior consultant blended day-rate (fractional benchmark)
economic_signals.implication_for_us: 5-day boutique project ≈ SGD 7.5-15k (~USD 5.6-11.2k). Elitez Vantage USD 1,500 starter is 4-7x cheaper for an arguably equivalent artefact — defendable price floor.

economic_signals.indicator: ChatGPT Pro / Claude Max annual seat cost
economic_signals.implication_for_us: Per-seat WTP ceiling. A 4-project annual relationship at USD 600/project beats a single ChatGPT Pro seat on price — a viable retainer entry point.

adoption_patterns.note: SME number reflects share that have purchased any CI / market-research artefact in the trailing 12 months (ASME 2025 + own triangulation). MNC number reflects share that have any active CI subscription or commissioned report. AI-research adoption is not the same as CI adoption — many SMEs use ChatGPT but only a minority commission a research deliverable.

trends.title: Deep Research at USD 200/mo collapses the 'generic CI deck' use case
trends.evidence: ChatGPT Pro 250 Deep Research runs/mo with o3-pro and 1M context (Apr 2026); Claude Research mode at USD 100-200; Manus 40k credits/mo for autonomous browse + code; Perplexity Max USD 200/mo. A literate buyer produces 70-80% of a competitor deck in 2-3 hours.
trends.implication_for_us: Compete on (i) accountability contract, (ii) SG-licensing, (iii) PSG funnel, (iv) methodology reproducibility. Do not compete on deck production speed.

trends.title: OSS agentic templates proliferate; Anthropic Skills marketplaces hit 4,200+
trends.evidence: claudemarketplaces.com lists 4,200+ skills, 770+ MCP servers, 2,500+ marketplaces (Apr 2026). CrewAI + LangGraph star counts crossed in early 2026. Qwen-Agent + Tencent Hunyuan provide Asia-region OSS.
trends.implication_for_us: Open-source the JSON schema (low marginal cost); keep the methodology docs and grant-fluency commercial. Substitution risk is highest in 12-24 months.

trends.title: SEA-source weighting is genuinely under-served by global CI
trends.evidence: AlphaSense, Crayon, Klue, Kompyte all skew US/EU. Cento, Momentum Works, Tech in Asia, DealStreetAsia provide SEA depth but only at sector-specific or investor lens — not as a CI deliverable. Asia Insight / Kadence SG do fieldwork but not template-shaped methodology.
trends.implication_for_us: Methodology + SEA quota + AI speed is a genuine three-axis differentiation. Make the SEA-quota rubric (≥60% SEA/APAC) a public marketing claim.

trends.title: Government grants are increasingly the procurement gate, not the discount
trends.evidence: PSG SG, EDG, WSG WDG(JR+) — SG SME procurement timelines now centre on grant-eligibility before vendor evaluation. Non-eligible vendors are filtered before pricing is even compared.
trends.implication_for_us: PSG pre-approval is a Y1 must-do; without it, the ~50% pricing edge AI subs offer wins on raw price.

trends.title: Methodology IP is the durable wedge in a fork-friendly ecosystem
trends.evidence: OSS templates can clone the JSON shape but cannot clone a 600-line FIELD-DICTIONARY + 9-agent specs + inter-agent quality gates without significant authorial work. Buyer Slack threads Apr 2026 cite 'the docs' as the differentiator.
trends.implication_for_us: Treat docs as IP. Provide JSON open-source as bait; sell the methodology license at the higher tier.

trends.title: AI-research buyer fatigue creates a window for editorial-design CI
trends.evidence: Founder Slack + Reddit Apr 2026 mock the 'ChatGPT-output deck' aesthetic. Editorial / serif design (Playfair + Inter, slate/amber/red) reads as 'human-curated' and gets read past page 1.
trends.implication_for_us: Brand investment in editorial design (Display: Playfair, Body: Inter, structured implication cards) is a concrete sales asset, not vanity.
